book_summary: 2 Thessalonians 1-3 has full chapter coverage with source-audited-draft restored translation and mystical companion layers. Verse-number alignment is preserved in both restored and mystical files. The book preserves endurance under pressure, Divine justice and relief, careful non-sensational day-of-Lord correction, the mystery of lawlessness handled without modern target-mapping, standing firm in tradition, shared responsibility, restorative discipline, and peace/grace for all.

chapters.notes: Growing trust and increasing love, endurance under pressure, righteous/just judgment, relief for pressured community, unveiling of Lord Jesus, glorified in holy ones, prayer for worthy calling and work of trust in power.

Day-of-Lord correction, do not be shaken, rebellion/departure, human of lawlessness, restrainer left unidentified, mystery of lawlessness already working, false signs/wonders, love of truth, stand firm, age-long comfort and good hope in grace.

Prayer for word to run, trustworthy Lord guarding, hearts directed into Divine love and Messiah endurance, disorderly idleness and meddling, apostolic work pattern, do not grow weary doing good, do not treat corrected one as enemy but admonish as sibling, final peace/grace.

translation_notes: 2 Thessalonians should preserve the pastoral purpose: steady a pressured community, correct panic, and encourage faithful practice.
Thlipsis is pressure/affliction; parousia presence/arrival; apokalypsis unveiling; anomia lawlessness; apostasia rebellion/departure; katechon restraining one/thing; paraklesis comfort/encouragement.
Chapter 2 must be handled non-sensationally. The lawless one and restraining force are not identified in the text; preserve ambiguity rather than mapping them onto modern targets.
Mystery of lawlessness names a hidden pattern already at work, not a license for paranoia or accusation.
Judgment imagery in chapter 1 is apocalyptic justice and relief, not permission for human vengeance.

The work/idleness instruction in chapter 3 addresses disorderly refusal and meddling in this community context; it must not be weaponized against the poor, disabled, exploited, or those unable to work.
Verse 3:15 is crucial: do not regard the disciplined person as enemy, but admonish as sibling.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
Verse-number alignment must remain intact in both restored and mystical companion files.
source_witness_audit_flags: 2 Thessalonians 2:1-12: second-pass review for apocalyptic vocabulary, restrainer ambiguity, lawless one identity, and reception-history misuse.
2 Thessalonians 3:6-15: second-pass pastoral ethics review to prevent misuse against vulnerable people while preserving communal responsibility.
next_actions: Begin next book according to project plan. Suggested next: 1 Timothy, with careful handling of pastoral-office language, women/teaching passages, household order, wealth warnings, and anti-domination safeguards.
Add glossary entries or reuse hooks: thlipsis, parousia, apokalypsis, anomia, apostasia, katechon, paraklesis, lawlessness, restrainer, stand firm, peace, and restorative discipline.
